nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef environments, typically found on hard substrates in the photic zone

decorPreferences: Attach to live rock via wedging, zip-tie/fishing line, or superglue
Do not bury in sand — red algae will melt at the base if buried

avoidWith: strong herbivorous fish that may consume macroalgae

imageCandidates.notes: Product page image from Mosaic Macros listing for String-of-Pearls (Coelarthrum sp.).

summary: An ornamental red macroalgae with distinctive grape-like, fluid-filled bladders resembling a string of pearls. Generally suited for display in reef tanks; tends to grow slowly and is typically kept attached to rockwork.
careNotes: Coelarthrum sp. 'String-of-Pearls' is a red macroalga (Rhodophyta) with characteristic round, fluid-filled bladders reminiscent of Botryocladia, but lacking a pronounced stolon. It is generally considered an intermediate-level display macroalgae. As a red alga, it must be attached to rock rather than planted in sand — placing it in a sand bed will cause the base to melt, per Mosaic Macros guidance. Recommended attachment methods include wedging between rocks, securing with zip-tie or fishing line (tied loosely to avoid damaging the thallus), or short-term superglue gel to a rock plug. Lighting should be full-spectrum; actinic-only spectra are generally inadequate. Flow should be gentle to moderate, similar to a soft-coral display tank — excessive flow has been known to tear delicate thalli. Weekly dosing of an all-around micronutrient supplement (iron, iodine, and trace elements) is advised. Calcium 400–450 ppm and magnesium 1250–1350 ppm targets help maintain a stable reef environment. Nitrate 5–20 ppm and phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm provide adequate nutrition for growth. Macroalgae sold by Mosaic Macros may arrive with hitchhiking organisms; inspect upon receipt. Adult size estimate (5–15 cm) is based on typical fragment sizes offered (4"+ cuttings) and general Coelarthrum growth habit; no precise published size data was located during research — reviewer may wish to verify.
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is primarily by fragmentation — breaking off healthy branch segments and re-attaching them to rock. Coelarthrum also undergoes gametophyte-sporophyte alternation in the wild, but this life-cycle phase is not typically managed in aquarium settings.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Provided water parameter targets (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm), lighting guidance (full-spectrum), flow guidance, and the sand-bed melt warning for red algae. Cross-verified as the authoritative secondary for Mosaic-only entries per playbook.
